carbon_policy_signal.item: EU Commission weakens methane enforcement rules and floats oil-firm exemptions on energy-security grounds; structural signal of climate-rule relaxation under geopolitical pressure.
carbon_policy_signal.why_it_matters: Exemptions reduce operational friction for fossil suppliers, supporting lower marginal gas cost and thermal generation economics; signals broader climate-rule erosion, depressing long-term EUA carry and structural carbon floor.

freshness_caveat: Coal data stale (133 days old, as of 2025-12-26); clean_dark stale (128 days old, as of 2025-12-31). Coal 8th percentile headline and clean_dark 50th percentile signals unreliable; use only for directional context. All other metrics current as of 2026-05-08 (GB Power, Clean Spark, DE Power, Renewables, Storage, TTF fresh; EUA 1 day old).
risk_flags: GB Power 126.73 EUR/MWh (93rd pctile) — Hormuz closure risk premium; US reassurance on patrol reduces near-term shock but Libya ramp-up remains uncertain.
Storage 34.51% full, 12.4 pp below seasonal average (11th pctile) — summer refill pace critical; Iranian disruption tightens LNG sourcing for EU replenishment.
Renewable share 27.07% (18th pctile, -32.82% daily swing) — thermal plant call surging; coal data stale (133 days old) but 8th pctile suggests merit-order compression favours gas-to-power.
EU methane exemptions in play — structural bear for long-term carbon; eases gas producer constraints and supports lower TTF ceiling.

base.summary: Hormuz patrol reassurance holds; Libya talks ongoing; storage refill gradual; renewables recovering. GB premium moderates to 80th pctile.

downside.summary: Iran ceasefire agreed; Libya ramps materially; renewables recovery accelerates; storage refill exceeds seasonal. Geopolitical unwind.

upside.summary: Hormuz chokepoint worsens or Iran escalates; Libya pipeline delays; LNG cargoes diverted. Geopolitical premium reasserts.

themes: Iran Hormuz disruption, Libya gas hedge, Libyan supply ramp uncertainty
Storage deficit pressuring summer refill, renewables cliff lifting thermal merit order
EU methane exemptions loosening fossil supply constraints, bearish long-term carbon
top_drivers: Iran Hormuz disruption lifting GB Power to 93rd percentile (126.73 EUR/MWh); US Hormuz patrol reassurance moderates immediate shock but Libya ramp-up remains key uncertainty.
Renewable collapse to 18th percentile (-32.82% daily) and storage 12.4 pp below seasonal forcing thermal coal/gas merit-order compression; clean spark 92nd pctile reflects gas-to-power premium.
EU methane exemptions softening fossil producer constraints, structurally bearish EUA (26th pctile) and long-term carbon carry; supports lower TTF ceiling if supply-easing succeeds.
top_takeaway: GB Power at 93rd percentile on Iran supply disruption; storage 12 pp below seasonal; renewable share collapsed to 18th percentile. Geopolitical premium dominant.

watchlist.metric: Hormuz strait reopening / Iran ceasefire timeline
watchlist.why: Direct transmission to global LNG/crude flows; Iran supply loss underpins GB 93rd-pctile premium and DE Power +31.87% monthly.

watchlist.metric: Libya gas field ramp-up and pipeline export rate to Italy
watchlist.why: Marginal LNG source for EU; success reduces geopolitical premium on TTF; failure extends Hormuz-driven supply tightness.

watchlist.metric: EU methane exemption issuance (oil firms, energy security carve-out)
watchlist.why: Signals climate-rule relaxation trajectory; structural bearish for EUA (26th pctile, -1.47% daily); eases fossil marginal cost.

watchlist.metric: Storage refill pace through June–July (German, French facilities)
watchlist.why: 11th pctile deficit + Iranian LNG shortage forces higher marginal cost curve; summer power risk if refill underperforms seasonal +4pp pace.

watchlist.metric: US tariff escalation on EU energy sector
watchlist.why: Trump tariff threat noted in watchlist; potential retaliatory impact on LNG import economics, refined product flows, power pricing.
